netn.AISRadioSignal.description: This is the root interaction class for all AIS messages. The interaction class contains a reference to the embedded system that represents the AIS transmitter. The embedded system should be modelled as a RadioTransmitter, an RPR-FOM object class that is subclassed from Embedded System.

hostRadioIndex.description: Optional (Default: not available). The HostRadioIndex is a unique string that identifies the name of the RadioTransmitter object.

netn.ATP45HazardArea.description: Represents the footprint generated by a CBRN warning and reporting simulation. This follows the NATO ATP-45 doctrine to generate a hazard area.

locations.description: Array of locations that define the hazard area

time.description: Simulation time at which this hazard area was generated.

uniqueID.description: Unique representation of the area’s ID.

validityTime.description: Duration (in seconds) from the Time attribute that this hazard area prediction is valid.

netn.ATP45HazardAreaTypeEnum8.description: Type of ATP-45 Hazard Area (simple or detailed).

netn.AcceptOffer.description: Use the AcceptOffer interaction to accept an offer received in an OfferService interaction.

requestId.description: Required: Unique identifier for the requested service related to the transaction.

netn.AcknowledgeData.description: Acknowledge data for Message Types 7 and 13.

hostRadioIndex.description: Optional (Default: not available). The HostRadioIndex is a unique string that identifies the name of the RadioTransmitter object.

userId.description: Required. The message is from the vessel identified by this MMSI.

netn.Activate.description: Request federate to change of status of AggregateUnit to Active. If required the unit will be registered in the federation.

aggregateUnit.description: Required for all requests except QuerySupportedCapabilities. Unique identifier for the NETN_Aggregate for which this request is related to.

eventId.description: Unique identifier for all MRM interactions belonging to the same request/respons event.

federate.description: Required. Intended federate responsible for performing the requested action. Sending federate should ensure that receiving federate can perform requested action. If not able to perform, a response interaction indicating failure should be returned.

netn.ActiveStatusEnum8.description: A state which indicates the status of an object concerning its participation in the simulation. An object in an inactive state is not simulated and does not interact with other objects.

netn.AddPassage.description: Tasking of an entity to lay/build a passage between the two given points. The passage can, for example, be a passage through an obstacle or a bridge over a river. The tasked entity should be within a certain distance tolerance (specified in the federation agreement) of one of the points of the passage. A passage object should be registered in the federation with the provided UUID.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

point1.description: Required. The line between point1 and point2 defines the geometry of the passage.

point2.description: Required. The line between point1 and point2 defines the geometry of the passage.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

uniqueId.description: Required. A unique UUID of the passage.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.AgentClassEnum8.description: Class of Agent for an ATP-45 Hazard Area.

netn.AgentConcentrationStruct.description: Concentration value associated with a specific agent.

netn.AgentMassStruct.description: Mass of contaminant inside a vehicle brought in by an embedded unit.

netn.Aggregate.description: Instruction to the AggregateFederate to perform aggregation of the specified AggregateUnit's parts.

aggregateUnit.description: Required for all requests except QuerySupportedCapabilities. Unique identifier for the NETN_Aggregate for which this request is related to.

eventId.description: Unique identifier for all MRM interactions belonging to the same request/respons event.

federate.description: Required. Intended federate responsible for performing the requested action. Sending federate should ensure that receiving federate can perform requested action. If not able to perform, a response interaction indicating failure should be returned.

removeSubunits.description: Optional. Indicates if the disaggregate subunits (represented as aggregates in the federation) should be deleted from the federation execution. Default is TRUE - all subunits should be deleted. If FALSE all disaggregate subunits shall be set to inactive.

netn.AggregateMissionEnum16.description: Representation of the general class or nature of activity related to a unit's mission. Enumerations are based on JC3IEDM action-event-category-code.

netn.AidToNavigation.description: This class defines additional attributes for aid to navigation (ATON) equipment.

netn.AidTypeEnumType.description: Enumeration value for the aid type. According to [IALA], the aid type field has values 1-15 for fixed and 16-31 for floating aids to navigation.

netn.AisBaseStationReportAndUTCDateRespo.description: Common reporting structure for Message Types 4 and 11 for reporting UTC time and date and, at the same time, position. It is also used by AIS stations for determining if the station is within 120 NM for response to Messages Types 20 and 23.

hostRadioIndex.description: Optional (Default: not available). The HostRadioIndex is a unique string that identifies the name of the RadioTransmitter object.

uTCtime.description: Optional (Default: not available). Time of report.

userId.description: Required. The message is from the vessel identified by this MMSI.

netn.AisBinaryMessageData.description: Binary Payload for Message Types 6 and 8. The interpretation of the binary payload is controlled by:  - The Designated Area Code (DAC), which is a jurisdiction code: 366 for the United States. It uses the same encoding as the area designator in MMMSIs; see [ITU-MID]. 1 designates international (ITU) messages.  - The FID, which is the Functional ID for a message subtype. In some sources this is abbreviated FI.

data.description: Required. Binary data up to 920 bits.

hostRadioIndex.description: Optional (Default: not available). The HostRadioIndex is a unique string that identifies the name of the RadioTransmitter object.

userId.description: Required. The message is from the vessel identified by this MMSI.

netn.AisEquipmentItem.description: This class defines additional attributes for AIS equipment. Depending on the kind of AIS equipment, further attributes are added in sub-classes.

mmsi.description: Optional. The MMSI number (Maritime Mobile Service Identity) of the AIS station.  If the value is not provided then the subscribing federate that is responsible for the modelling of the AIS station shall generate a value. Note that the value of each individual AIS station must be  unique across all AIS stations in the simulation. Recycling MMSI numbers is not adviced since some systems may retain dead tracks for some time.

radioSystemType.description: Optional. The type of transmitter. If the value is not provided then the subscribing federate that is responsible for the modelling of the AIS station shall determine the type of transmitter.

transmitterStatus.description: Optional. The initial status of the AIS Transmitter(s) of the AIS station. If the value is not provided then the value shall assumed to be ON.

netn.AisMessage.description: Super class for all AIS message types.

hostRadioIndex.description: Optional (Default: not available). The HostRadioIndex is a unique string that identifies the name of the RadioTransmitter object.

userId.description: Required. The message is from the vessel identified by this MMSI.

netn.AisMessage1.description: Message Type 1: Position Report Class A.    Message type for a scheduled position report; Class A shipborne mobile equipment. This message transmits information pertaining to a ships navigation: Longitude and latitude, time, heading, speed, ships navigation status (under power, at anchor...). This message is transmitted every 2 to 10 seconds while underway, and every 3 minutes while at anchor.

courseOverGround.description: Optional (Default: not available). Course over ground.

The value of this parameter is also encoded and used as the User Supplied Tag in the HLA Ownership services to allow a correlation between the TMR interactions and HLA Ownership services. The `EventId` is generated by a trigger federate or, if no trigger federate is used, directly by the requesting federate. The value of this parameter should match all related TMR pattern interactions belonging to the same Transfer of Modelling Responsibilities event.

reason.description: Optional. An enumerated value that describes the reason for the cancellation: `Other` = When a Requesting Federate for some reason except time out decides not to complete a transfer. `TimeOut` = When a model's time limit for receiving a TMR offer has passed.

netn.CheckPoint.description: A checkpoint defines a location where simulated aggregate and physical entities should stop and wait a specified time before continuing on their route.  If the checkpoint is currently in operation the status attribute should be set to true. A system simulating movement of physical or aggregate entities can be either directly affected by the checkpoint by subscribing to its state or indirectly by receiving an NETN-ETR `Wait` task.

delayTime.description: Required. The time that an entity shall wait at the checkpoint before passing. The time is a nominal value; federates can use this for modifying delay time for different types of entities, e.g. add or subtract a value or multiply with a type depending factor.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

This corresponds to the CommunicationNet-Elements of MSDL Units and Equipments.  Instances of this object class should be considered as optional. No federate should rely on this data to work with communication networks.

networkType.description: Optional. The communication network type of use.

serviceType.description: Optional. The type of service used on the communication network.

uniqueName.description: Required. Unique communication network name. Unique in the context of communication networks.

netn.CommunicationNode.description: A CommunicationNode is the representation of the interface of a simulated entity to logical communication networks. The location of the CommunicationNode is derived from the referenced entity or specified explicitly (if the referenced entity is not registered in the federation).    Connections to communication networks are established only if connectivity exists based on the status of available network devices and the simulation of links. Each potential connection is described in terms of requested connections  (the equivalent to the information provided by MSDL).

entityId.description: Required. Reference by UUID to an object instance of one of the following classes:    * NETN-MRM Aggregate Entity  * NETN-Physical extensions of RPR Physical Entities.    If the referenced entity exists in the federation, the location of the node is derived from the location of the entity.  If the referenced entity does not exist in the federation, the location of the node is defined by the Location attribute.

incomingConnections.description: Optional. Description of all incoming connections to the receiving entity.

location.description: Optional. Specifies the location of the CommunicationNode in case the entity referenced by EntityId is not registered in the federation. netn.EnvironmentCondition.description: Root class for all types of EnvironmentCondition objects.    The region where the EnvironmentCondition applies is defined by the GeoReference attribute, as follows.    If GeoReference is provided as a geographical area, and the sub class in question has surface semantics (water/land), the EnvironmentCondition applies only within that area of the surface.    If GeoReference is provided as a geographical area, and the sub class in question defines a Layer attribute, the EnvironmentCondition applies only within the volume bounded both by the Layer and by the body described by projecting each point of the area along a line through the center of the earth. (This means, e.g., that any volumes described by two adjacent geodetic quadrangles will never overlap, and if they belong to the same layer, will also be adjacent.)    If GeoReference is provided as a RPREntityReference, NETNEntityReference or GMLFeatureReference identifying a feature (as opposed to a geometry object), the EnvironmentCondition is considered to apply only to the immediate vicinity of the referenced entity/feature: any Layer attribute specified by a sub class is ignored in this case.    If not provided, the EnvironmentCondition is considered global, only restricted by the semantics of its particular sub class.

During the execution of the task, a CheckPoint object (NETN-SE) should be registered in the federation. The completion and operational status of the CheckPoint object should be updated continuously during the execution of the task or at task completion. Immediately after the checkpoint has been established, it will be operated by the tasked entity for a specified duration of time.

reportId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the report.

weaponType.description: Required. The type of the weapon that is in range.

when.description: Required. Date and time when the reported status was valid.

netn.IncomingConnectionStruct.description: Characteristics of a specific incoming connection.

bandwidth.description: Amount of data per time unit.

communicationNetworkName.description: Reference to the communication network associated with the incoming connection.

hopCount.description: The number of hops in physical network required for sender entity to reach receiver entity.

reliability.description: Reliability of connection in terms of availability. 100% = always available and no connection loss.

senderEntityId.description: Reference by UUID to the simulated entity sending on the connection.

netn.InitiateTransfer.description: An `InitiateTransfer` interaction is sent by a **Trigger Federate** to initiate a TMR request. Parameters include information on the participating federates, which object instances and what attributes are included in the transfer.  The **Trigger Federate** shall generate a unique `EventId` to be used in all subsequent TMR interactions related to the event.

attributes.description: Required. The common set of attributes for all referenced instances to be included in the transfer. Reference to attributes is by attribute name.

eventId.description: Required. The `EventId` is a unique reference to a specific execution of a TMR pattern. It consists of two values, a Counter and a Federate Handle. The value of this parameter is also encoded and used as the User Supplied Tag in the HLA Ownership services to allow a correlation between the TMR interactions and HLA Ownership services. The `EventId` is generated by a trigger federate or, if no trigger federate is used, directly by the requesting federate. The value of this parameter should match all related TMR pattern interactions belonging to the same Transfer of Modelling Responsibilities event.

instances.description: Required. References (UUID) to NETN Platform, Lifeform or Aggregate instances included in the transfer.

requestFederate.description: Required. A name specifying the federate requesting the transfer.

responseFederate.description: Required unless TransferType is AquireWithoutNegotiation: A Federate Name specifying the federate that is the responding federate.

transferType.description: Required. An enumerated value that indicates the direction of the transfer of the ownership and whether there shall be any negotiation: `Acquire` = Requesting Federate acquires instance attribute ownership from the Responding Federate (current owner), `Divest` = Requesting Federate (current owner) releases instance attribute ownership to Responding Federate and `AcquireWithoutNegotiating` = Requesting Federate acquires unowned instance attributes.

netn.Installation.description: Representation of an installation/facility based on attributes defined in the NETN-ORG extensions of MSDL.

entityType.description: Required. SISO-REF-010 code for entity type definitions. If unknown, use 0.0.0.0.0.0.0.

location.description: Required. Geographic location of the installation.

nameNonSen.description: Required. A unique name of the installation.

owner.description: Required. Reference to the force, unit or equipment item owning or hosting the installation.

symbolAmplification.description: Optional. Text amplifications for the installation's symbol. Static or very low update rate.

symbolId.description: Required. A symbol identifier represented as a string.

additionalInformation.description: A text amplifier for units, equipment and installations.

combatEffectiveness.description: A text amplifier for units and installations that indicates unit effectiveness or installation capability.

engagementBarText.description: A text amplifier placed immediately atop the symbol in the engagement bar. May denote, 1) local/remote status; 2) engagement status; and 3) weapon type.

evaluationRating.description: A text amplifier for units, equipment and installations that consists of a one-letter reliability rating and a one-number credibility rating.    Reliability Ratings:  A-completely reliable  B-usually reliable  C-fairly reliable  D-not usually reliable  E-unreliable  F-reliability cannot be judged    Credibility Ratings:  1-confirmed by other sources  2-probably true  3-possibly true  4-doubtfully true  5-improbable  6-truth cannot be judged.

iffSifAis.description: A text amplifier displaying IFF/SIF/AIS identification modes and codes.

staffComments.description: A text amplifier for units, equipment and installations.

uniqueDesignation.description: A text amplifier for units, equipment and installations that uniquely identifies a particular symbol or track number. Identifies acquisitions number when used with SIGINT symbology.

netn.LOGInteraction.description: Base class for all NETN Logistics Pattern Service Transactions.

requestId.description: Required: Unique identifier for the requested service related to the transaction.

netn.LandSurface.description: The environment conditions related to a land surface.  Besides the common attributes such as Temperature, Wind and Precipitaion etc. additional detail regarding snow, moisture, and ice conditions of the land surface can be represented.  All attributes of LandSurfaceCondition objects are optional.  An instance of this class without a GeoReference is considered to apply to all land surfaces.

iceCondition.description: Optional. Surface ice condition in the region/location specified by the EnvironmentCondition. Default is no ice.  If EnvironmentConditions with overlapping regions/locations exist, surface ice condition is calculated as the highest enumerated value making the resulting value the one with the most severe ice condition.

moisture.description: Optional. Surface moisture in the region/location specified by the EnvironmentCondition. Default is 0 (Dry).  If EnvironmentConditions with overlapping regions/locations exist, moisture is calculated as the highest enumerated value making the resulting value the one with most moisture.

snow.description: Optional. Current snow depth and density in the region/location specified by the EnvironmentCondition. Default is no snow.  If EnvironmentConditions with overlapping regions/locations exist the average snow depth and density should be used.

netn.LandSurfaceCondition.description: Response with condition for land surface.

barometricPressure.description: Optional. Average barometric pressure measured in millibar or hectopascal (1 mbar = 1hPa) in the region/location.

environmentObjectId.description: Optional. Reference to an existing environment condition object if the corresponding request includes UpdateAsObject set to true.

eventId.description: Required: Unique identifier of the request. Will be referenced in a resulting resulting METOC_Response interaction.

geoReference.description: Optional. Geographical reference to indicate for which point, area, path or object this request is related to. Default if not provided the environment condition data is global.

haze.description: Optional. Average haze density in the region/location,

humidity.description: Optional. Average humidity in percent in the region/location.

iceCondition.description: Optional. Most severe ice condition in the region/location.

moisture.description: Optional. Maximum surface moisture in the region/location.

precipitation.description: Optional. Average precipitation intensisty region/location for a precipitation type in the following order of precedence Snow, Hail, Rain, NoPrecipitation.

snow.description: Optional. Average snow depth and density in the region/location.

status.description: Required: Specifies the result of the request action. TRUE indicates success.

temperature.description: Optional. Average temperature in the region/location.

visibilityRange.description: Optional. Average distance at which an object or light can be clearly discerned by the human eye in the region/location.

wind.description: Optional. Average wind speed and direction in the region/location.

netn.LayerStruct.description: A layer of the Earth's environment expressed as a base altitude and a thickness.

base.description: The altitude of the bottom end of the layer, in meters above mean sea level (MSL). Negative values means the base is below mean sea level.

thickness.description: Thickness of the layer in meters above the base altitude. Always a positive value.

netn.LinkStates.description: Representation of the state of links in a physical network.

links.description: Required. Status of a set of physical network links.

physicalNetworkName.description: Required. Reference to physical network by its unique name.

netn.LinkStatusArray.description: The status of physical network links.

netn.LinkStatusStruct.description: Status of a physcial network link.

bandwidth.description: Amount of data per time unit.

isBidirectional.description: True is link is bi-directional. By default this value should be set to false.

reliability.description: Reliability of connection in terms of availability. 100% = always available and no connection loss.

netn.Location.description: A location is a geographical point in latitude, longitude and altitude above mean sea level.

point.description: Required. A geographical point in latitude, longitude, and altitude above mean sea level.

netn.METOCInteraction.description: Root class for requesting environment data from a METOC Service.

eventId.description: Required: Unique identifier of the request. Will be referenced in a resulting resulting METOC_Response interaction.

netn.METOCRoot.description: Superclass for Environment Conditions and Layers.

nameNonSen.description: Optional. Name of the Environment Condition or METOC Service.

uniqueId.description: Required. Unique identifier for the Environment Condition object or METOC Service.

netn.MRMInteraction.description: Base class for all MRM interactions.

eventId.description: Unique identifier for all MRM interactions belonging to the same request/respons event.

netn.MagicMove.description: A simulation control task that instructs the entity to immediately jump to the specified location and update the new position and heading.

location.description: Required. Location of the entity.

locationUuid.description: Optional. The new location for the entity. Refered by a UUID.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the simulation control task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that should execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Indentifies the commander of the task.

netn.MagicResource.description: A simulation control task that instructs the entity to set amount of available resources immediately

resource.description: Required. Defines the resource type that will get a new quantity value.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the simulation control task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that should execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Indentifies the commander of the task.

netn.ManeuverIndicatorEnumType.description: Enumeration value to indicate a maneuver.

netn.ManufacturerIdType.description: The manufacturer mnemonic code consisting of three 6 bit ASCII characters.

netn.Merge.description: Instruction to merg the simulated AggregateUnit with the selected divided parts. After successful merge the divided parts are removed from the federation and their resources are combined with the AggregatedUnit.

aggregateUnit.description: Required for all requests except QuerySupportedCapabilities. Unique identifier for the NETN_Aggregate for which this request is related to.

eventId.description: Unique identifier for all MRM interactions belonging to the same request/respons event.

federate.description: Required. Intended federate responsible for performing the requested action. Sending federate should ensure that receiving federate can perform requested action. If not able to perform, a response interaction indicating failure should be returned.

subunits.description: Required. A set of unique identifiers of subelements of the AggregateUnit. These can be any subunit and/or equipment defined at a subunit on any level.

netn.MineCountVariantStruct.description: The number or density of mines.

netn.MissionStruct.description: The operational task the aggregate has been ordered to perform, the time the mission was assigned, and the estimated completion time.

endTime.description: An optional field providing the mission estimated end time

missionEnum.description: The mission assigned to the aggregate

startTime.description: An optional field providing the mission start time

netn.ModelResolutionTypeEnum32.description: Enumeration indicating the level of fidelity appropriate for instancing the unit or equipment in the simulation.

netn.Mount.description: Tasking of entity to mount the specified entity. The tasked entity should be within a certain distance (tolerance specified in the federation agreements) of the location of the entity to mount.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

entityId.description: Required. Reference to the entity to mount.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.Move.description: Tasking of an entity to move in a specified direction for a given duration of time.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

duration.description: Required. The duration of the move.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.MoveIntoFormation.description: Tasking of an entity to move into the given formation on the given location with the given heading.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

distance.description: Required. The minimum distance between entities in the formation.

formation.description: Required. The type of positional arrangement the entity.

heading.description: Required. The heading of the formation.

location.description: Required. The location to move to.

locationUuid.description: Optional. The location to move to by reference to a NETN SE, SE_GeoObject.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.MoveToEntity.description: Tasking of an entity to move to another entity. If a path is provided, the entity should follow the path as its route to the entity. The entity should align with the path from its current position to the nearest position or waypoint on the path. The entity should leave the path at position or waypoint on the path closest to the destination entity. The entity moves directly towards the destination entity if no path (or a zero-length path) is provided.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

entity.description: Required. Reference to the entity to move to.

moveType.description: Required. Indicates if roads have to be followed.

path.description: Optional. Route to the specified entity.

pathUuid.description: Optional. Path to use to get to the Location by reference to a NETN_SE, SE_GeoObject.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.MoveToLocation.description: Tasking of an entity to move to the specified destination location. If a path is provided, the entity should follow the path as its route to the destination. The entity should align with the path from its current position to the nearest position or waypoint on the path. The entity should leave the path at position or waypoint on the path closest to the destination. The entity moves directly towards the destination location if no path (or a zero-length path) is provided.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

location.description: Required. The location to move to.

locationUuid.description: Optional. The location to move to, by reference to a NETN_SE, SE_GeoObject.

moveType.description: Required. Indicates if roads have to be followed.

path.description: Optional. A route to use in order to move to the Location.

pathUuid.description: Optional. Path to use to get to the Location by reference to a NETN_SE, SE_GeoObject.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.MoveTypeEnum32.description: CrossCountry: move directly to the destination without taking into account the roads.  OnlyRoads: stay on the roads to get to the closest point to the destination that is still on a road; if there is no road very near to the start (within around 10 meters) there will be no movement at all.  RoadsAndCrossCountry: move to the destination by taking into account the roads; it is allowed to go off the road.

netn.MsgIdEnumType.description: Enumeration value for the message type.

activity.description: Optional. The current activity of the platform. The value is based on the Joint Consultation, Command and Control Information Exchange Data Model (JC3IEDM) action-event-category-code. The JC3IEDM is a fully documented standard [NATO STANAG 5525] for an information exchange data model for the sharing of C2 information. Default is 0 (Other activity).

callsign.description: Required. A callsign used to address the unit. Callsigns should be unique in the context in which they are used but not required to be globally unique.

captureStatus.description: The status of an aggregate with respect to its control or influence over its own activities.

combatValue.description: Optional. A summary value (in per cent) of unit effectiveness based on the level of training, leadership, morale, personnel and equipment operational status, etc. The default value is 100%.

coverStatus.description: Optional. Describes the unit's protection from the effects of weapons fire. Default is 0% - Fully affected by weapon fire.

destination.description: Optional. The current destination of movement.

dividedUnitList.description: Optional. Reference to other aggregate or physical entities divided from the aggregate unit to represent specific subsets of holdings. If not published, a division is not supported.

echelon.description: Optional. The size of the unit (level of command).

electronicSignature.description: Describes the aggregate's susceptibility to electronic detection both as a summary value and by identifying aggregate sensors together with their operational status.

embeddedUnitList.description: Optional. Reference to units or platforms embarked on and transported by this unit. If not published, transport of embedded units not supported.

entityList.description: Optional. This attribute provides data on all entities comprising the aggregate. Entities include equipment, e.g. platforms, weapons, sensors and lifeforms such as personnel. Each entity contains key status attributes and subunit allocation information. If not provided the status and allocation of entities is not modelled on an entity level.

equipmentStatus.description: Optional. This summarizes the health status of the equipment comprising the aggregate. If not provided, the status of equipment is undefined.

hUMINTSignature.description: Describes the unit's susceptibility to human intelligence (HUMINT), i.e. information collected and provided by human sources.

higherHeadquarters.description: Optional. A reference to an entity representing the aggregate unit's superior or headquarters from which orders are given and to which reports are sent. The highest level unit or headquarters will publish 0000000000000000 as its HigherHeadquarters value. The referenced entity may or may not be registered in the federation as a NETN_Aggregate and/or NETN-ORG unit. If not published, the aggregate does not have a superior unit or headquarter. The default value is 0000000000000000 (no higher headquarters).

mission.description: The operational task the aggregate has been ordered to perform.

mounted.description: Optional. The percentage of aggregate personnel travelling on or in their organic transport. Default 100% - all personnel mounted.

parentUnit.description: Optional. Reference to parent aggregate entity. If not published, aggregation is not supported. The default value is 0000000000000000 (no parent unit).

personnelStatus.description: Optional. This summarizes the health status of personnel comprising the aggregate. If not provided, the status of personnel is undefined.

route.description: Optional. The current path of movement.

sourceUnit.description: Optional. Reference to an active NETN_Aggregate instance, the source of a NETN-MRM division. If not published, merging is not supported. The default value is 0000000000000000 representing no source unit.

status.description: Required. Indicate if this aggregate unit currently is being simulated or not. E.g. units mounted or embarked on transports can be set to inactive. During an inactive state, the attribute values may not reflect an accurate, current value. Therefore, any subscribing federate can ignore inactive units. An inactive instance may have its instance attributes updated by a federate but reflected updates shall be ignored by receiving federates. All attributes must be updated to represent the current status of the instance before setting the status to active.

subunitList.description: Optional. Reference to disaggregated representations of subsets of the aggregate unit when registered in the federation. Each element should refer to an existing NETN_Aggregate object in the federation. If not published, disaggregation is not supported.

suppliesStatus.description: Optional. The type and quantities of supplies available (on hand) to the unit. If not provided, the amount of available supplies is undefined.

symbolId.description: Optional. A symbol identifier represented as a string.

uniqueId.description: Required. A unique identifier for the object. The Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) is either generated or defined as part of scenario initialization, e.g. using NETN-ORG MSDL data. The unique identifier can serve dual purposes. It is a unique identification of the NETN_Aggregate object instance but can also be a reference to a NETN-ORG unit element with the same unique identifier.

visualSignature.description: Describes the unit's susceptibility to electro-optical detection.

weaponsControlOrder.description: Optional. Describes current Weapon Control Order Free, Tight, or Hold. Default is 0 - Other.

All attributes must be updated to represent the current status of the instance before status is set to active.

symbolId.description: Optional. A symbol identifier represented as a string.

uniqueId.description: Required. A unique identifier for the object. The Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) is either generated or defined as part of scenario initialization, e.g. using NETN-ORG MSDL data for defining specific platforms as equipment assigned to units.

activity.description: Optional. The current activity of the platform. The value is based on the Joint Consultation, Command and Control Information Exchange Data Model (JC3IEDM) action-event-category-code. The JC3IEDM is a fully documented standard [NATO STANAG 5525] for an information exchange data model for the sharing of C2 information. The default value is 0 (Other activity).

callsign.description: Required. A callsign used to address the platform. Callsigns should be unique in the context in which they are used but not required to be globally unique.

destination.description: Optional. The current destination of movement.

embeddedUnitList.description: Optional. Reference to platforms or lifeforms embarked on this platform. If not published, transport of embedded units is not supported. Entities are referenced by UUID. E.g. when soldiers are transported in a NETN Physical entity, then references (UUIDs) to these soldier instances are added to the EmbeddedUnitList for the entity. When a soldier disembarks from the transporter, the reference (UUID) to that soldier is removed from the EmbeddedUnitList. The default value is an empty list.

route.description: Optional. The current path of movement.

sourceUnit.description: Optional. Reference to an active NETN_Aggregate instance from which this physical entity was divided. If not published, merging is not supported. The default value is 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 (UUID with all zeros) representing no source unit.

status.description: Required. Indicate if this platform currently is being simulated or not. E.g. platforms mounted or embarked on transports can be set to inactive. netn.NavigationStatusEnumType.description: Enumeration value to indicate the navigational status.

netn.NetworkDeviceEmptyCharactersticsStr.description: Empty placeholder for future network device characteristics.

netn.NetworkDeviceGenericTransmitterChar.description: Generic description of network device transmitter.

maxLinkRange.description: Range limit for the link in meters. By default this value should be set to zero. If zero the max link range is based on the default value from the associated physical network.

netn.NetworkDeviceReceiverCharacteristic.description: Characteristics of a network device receiver.

netn.NetworkDeviceStruct.description: Describes the type and capability of a network device. Each device must have at least a transmitter (TX) or receiver (RX). Transceivers should define both.

communicationNetworks.description: Reference to all communication networks this device is used for.

isRelay.description: True if this network device acts as a relay. By default this value should be set to true.

networkDeviceModel.description: Optional name of the model of network device (e.g. a radio model name).

physicalNetworkName.description: Reference to physical network this device is connected to.

rx.description: Receiver characteristics of the network device.

tx.description: Transmitter characteristics of the network device.

netn.NoOfferReasonEnumType.description: Describes the reason why not accepting a TMR request

netn.ORGRoot.description: Root object class for all NETN-ORG object classes. Includes unique identifier and unique name attributes inherited by all subclasses.

uniqueId.description: Required. Unique Identifier.

netn.Observe.description: Tasking of an entity to observe an area.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.OfferRepair.description: Is sent by a federate simulating the service providing entity in response to a RequestRepair interaction.

offerTimeOut.description: Optional: The system wallclock time (in seconds after 1 January 1970) when the offer stops being valid. If not provided, the offer valid until otherwise specified.

offerType.description: Optional: Specifies if the offer is Negative (no Offer), Positive (Complete as requested) or Modified (not the same as the requested).

providerEntity.description: Required: Unique identifier of a simulated entity intended to perform the service. If not provided, the entity performing the service is considered unknown.

repairData.description: Required: List of the type of repairs offered. May be different from the list of requested repairs. List of all offered repairs if isOffering = true otherwise Undefined

requestId.description: Required: Unique identifier for the requested service related to the transaction.

startAppointment.description: Optional: Time and location of the start of service delivery.

netn.OfferService.description: The OfferService is a response to a RequestService. Subclasses of this interaction for specific types of offers contain a more detailed description of the offer. This information may include when, where, how the service can be delivered.

offerTimeOut.description: Optional: The system wallclock time (in seconds after 1 January 1970) when the offer stops being valid. If not provided, the offer valid until otherwise specified.

offerType.description: Optional: Specifies if the offer is Negative (no Offer), Positive (Complete as requested) or Modified (not the same as the requested).

providerEntity.description: Required: Unique identifier of a simulated entity intended to perform the service. If not provided, the entity performing the service is considered unknown.

requestId.description: Required: Unique identifier for the requested service related to the transaction.

startAppointment.description: Optional: Time and location of the start of service delivery.

netn.OfferSupply.description: Used by a supply service provider to indicate which of the requested materiel (amount and type) can be offered. In this request the consumer decides whether the loading is done by the provider or by the consumer.

offerTimeOut.description: Optional: The system wallclock time (in seconds after 1 January 1970) when the offer stops being valid. If not provided, the offer valid until otherwise specified.

offerType.description: Optional: Specifies if the offer is Negative (no Offer), Positive (Complete as requested) or Modified (not the same as the requested).

providerEntity.description: Required: Unique identifier of a simulated entity intended to perform the service. If not provided, the entity performing the service is considered unknown.

requestId.description: Required: Unique identifier for the requested service related to the transaction.

startAppointment.description: Optional: Time and location of the start of service delivery.

suppliesData.description: Required: List of type and quantity of supplies offered. May be different from the list of requested supplies. All offered supplies if isOffering = true otherwise Undefined

netn.OfferTransfer.description: An `OfferTransfer` interaction is sent by a **Response Federate** as a reply to a `RequestTransfer` to indicate if a transfer can be accomplished or not.    **Special Case**:  When the transfer type is Divesting, the delivery order of the interaction `OfferTransfer` and the HLA ownership callback service `requestAttributeOwnershipRelease` at the receiving federate is not determined. Due to this, receiving a `requestAttributeOwnershipRelease` callback should be treated in the same way as receiving a `OfferTransfer` with a positive offer. The user-supplied tag in the callback contains the `EventId` which identifies a specific transfer process.

eventId.description: Required. The `EventId` is a unique reference to a specific execution of a TMR pattern. It consists of two values, a Counter and a Federate Handle. The value of this parameter is also encoded and used as the User Supplied Tag in the HLA Ownership services to allow a correlation between the TMR interactions and HLA Ownership services. The `EventId` is generated by a trigger federate or, if no trigger federate is used, directly by the requesting federate. The value of this parameter should match all related TMR pattern interactions belonging to the same Transfer of Modelling Responsibilities event.

isOffering.description: Required. True if and only if for all instances all attributes in the request are offered. False if for any instance any attribute in the request is not offered.

reason.description: Optional. An enumerated value that shall specify the reason for a negative offer.

netn.OfferTransport.description: An Offer for a Transport support. The OfferTransport interaction shall be sent by the service providing federate in response to a RequestTransport interaction.

endAppointment.description: Optional: Location and time for disembarkment.

offerTimeOut.description: Optional: The system wallclock time (in seconds after 1 January 1970) when the offer stops being valid. If not provided, the offer valid until otherwise specified.

offerType.description: Optional: Specifies if the offer is Negative (no Offer), Positive (Complete as requested) or Modified (not the same as the requested).

providerEntity.description: Required: Unique identifier of a simulated entity intended to perform the service. If not provided, the entity performing the service is considered unknown.

requestId.description: Required: Unique identifier for the requested service related to the transaction.

startAppointment.description: Optional: Time and location of the start of service delivery.

netn.OfferTypeEnum32.description: Type of the offer (With restriction, positive, negative)

netn.OperateCheckPoint.description: Tasking of an entity to operate a checkpoint. The tasked entity should be within a certain distance (tolerance specified in the federation agreements) of the location of the checkpoint. If not, a separate move task should be issued first. The tasked entity activates an inactive checkpoint and operates the checkpoint for the specified duration of time.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

duration.description: Required. The duration of the operation of the checkpoint.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

uniqueId.description: Required. Reference to the checkpoint to operate.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.PartNumberEnumType.description: Identifier for the message part number.

netn.Path.description: A path is defiend as a sequence of at least two locations. Each location in the path is defined geodetic coordinates and altitude above the mean sea level.

points.description: Required. A path with at least 2 points expressed as GeodeticPoints.

netn.PathTypeEnum32.description: Specifies if a path is defined by waypoints or by reference to a path object in the federation.

netn.PathVariantStruct.description: Defines a path, either as a polygonal chain of waypoints or a UUID that refers to a path object in the federation.

netn.Patrol.description: Tasking of an entity to perform a patrol. The tasked entity moves from its current position to the start of the patrol route and then moves according to patrol route from its start point in the path, through all waypoints.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

moveType.description: Required. Defines how the entity shall move during the patrol route.

path.description: Required. Specifies the route to patrol.

pathUuid.description: Optional. Path for the patrol by reference to a NETN_SE, SE_GeoObject.

patrolType.description: Required. Defines how the patrol should be excuted. The full movement from start point, through the patrol route and back to the start point is called one cycle.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.PatrolMoveTypeEnum32.description: Defines how the movment shall be done during the execution of the patrol.  Other - Federate application specific.

netn.PatrolRepeating.description: Tasking of an entity to repeat a patrol for a given duration. When the duration time has passed, then the last cycle of the patrol is completed before the task ends. If the time of a cycle takes longer then the interval time, then the cycle starts directly (without delay). If the time of a cycle takes less then the interval time, then the entity waits at the first point of the patrol route until the next cycle is started.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

duration.description: Required. The duration of the total patrol task.

intervalTime.description: Required. The time between the start of two cycles, i.e. the time to complete a cycle and any waiting time before starting the next cycle.

moveType.description: Required. Defines how the entity shall move during the patrol route.

path.description: Required. Specifies the route to patrol.

pathUuid.description: Optional. Path for the patrol by reference to a NETN_SE, SE_GeoObject.

patrolType.description: Required. Defines how the patrol should be excuted. The full movement from start point, through the patrol route and back to the start point is called one cycle.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

why.description: Optional. A text describing the reason for this task.

netn.PatrolTypeEnum32.description: Other - The behaviour is federate application specific (e.g. wait for next command, etc.)  Circle - At the end of route, move to start point of patrol route (federate application determines the route)  Reverse - Follow the patrol route in reverse order.

netn.PhysicalGenericNetworkStruct.description: Characteristics of a generic physical network.

bandwidth.description: Amount of data per time unit.

defaultMaxLinkRange.description: The maximum distance between sending and receiving entities in a physical network link.

maxHopCount.description: The maximum nuber of hops allowed in the physical network.

reliability.description: Reliability of connection in terms of availability. 100% = always available and no connection loss.

netn.PhysicalNetwork.description: This object class represents type-specific parameters/bounds for a physical network. Physical networks are simulated by LinkState objects and do not require that an instance of PhysicalNetwork exist in the federation.

description.description: Required. Characteristics of the physical network.

uniqueName.description: Required. Unique physical network name. Uniqueness in the context of physical networks.

netn.PhysicalNetworkDescriptionVariant.description: Characteristics of physical networks depending on their type.

netn.PhysicalNetworkTypeEnum.description: The type of a physical network.

netn.PhysicalUndefinedNetworkStruct.description: (Empty) Characteristics of an undefined physical network.

netn.PointTypeEnum32.description: Specifies if a point is defined by a location or by reference to a point object in the federation.

netn.PointVariantStruct.description: Defines the point, either a Location or a UUID reference to a point object in the federation.

netn.PositionStatusReport.description: Report on a unit's position, speed, and heading.

comments.description: Optional. Any additional comments associated with the report.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer report messages. If not provided, the report transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the report messages directly.

entityId.description: Required. The entity that is reported about.

position.description: Required. Position of the entity at the specified time.

reportId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the report.

when.description: Required. Date and time when the reported status was valid.

intensity.description: Amount of precipitation in mm/per hour.

netn.ProbabilityHazardContourGroup.description: Represents the footprint covered by a CBRN hazard. This object covers the agent-specific effects which would be the output from a casualty model.

contours.description: Array of contours. These should be ordered in a sequence of ascending PercentProbabilityLevel.

time.description: Simulation time at which this contour group was generated.

uniqueID.description: Unique representation of the contour’s ID.

netn.ProbabilityHazardContourStruct.description: Represents the footprint covered by a CBRN hazard. This object covers the agent-specific effects which would be the output from a casualty model.

locations.description: Array of locations that define the probability contour.

percentProbabilityLevel.description: Percentage of entities within this contour being affected to the define exposure level.

netn.ProtectionEffectivenessStruct.description: Protection effectiveness associated with a specific agent.

effectiveness.description: Percentage by which this protection reduces effects.

netn.QuerySupportedCapabilitiesETR.description: Query which tasks, reports and SimCon actions that an entity supports. The queried entity shall respond with a CapabilitiesSupported message.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the simulation control task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that should execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Indentifies the commander of the task.

netn.QuerySupportedCapabilitiesMRM.description: A request to query the capabilities of a specified federation to provide support for MRM events. The queried federate shall respond with a CapabilitiesSupported interaction.

aggregateUnit.description: Required for all requests except QuerySupportedCapabilities. Unique identifier for the NETN_Aggregate for which this request is related to.

eventId.description: Unique identifier for all MRM interactions belonging to the same request/respons event.

federate.description: Required. Intended federate responsible for performing the requested action. Sending federate should ensure that receiving federate can perform requested action. If not able to perform, a response interaction indicating failure should be returned.

netn.RawDataHazardContourGroup.description: Represents the footprint covered by a CBRN hazard. This object covers the raw data which would be the output from a dispersion model.

contours.description: Array of contours. These should be ordered in a sequence of ascending ExposureLevel.

time.description: Simulation time at which this contour group was generated.

uniqueID.description: Unique representation of the contour’s ID.

netn.RawDataHazardContourStruct.description: Contour locations bounding a given exposure value.

exposureLevel.description: Quantity of exposure, measured in SI units.

locations.description: Array of locations that define the hazard contour.

netn.ReadyToReceiveService.description: Issued by a service consumer to indicate that service delivery can start. The time of service delivery may be significantly later than indicating ready for service delivery.

requestId.description: Required: Unique identifier for the requested service related to the transaction.

netn.Region.description: A region is an area on the earth's surface defined by a polygon or a circle.

area.description: Required. Defines the area, either as a geodetic polygon, circle or quadrangle.

netn.RejectOffer.description: Used to reject an offer made by a service providing entity as indicated in a OfferService interaction. By issuing a RejectOffer interaction the service consuming entity informs the providing entity that the offer has been rejected.

reason.description: Optional: Allows to inform about the reason of the cancel (free text)

requestId.description: Required: Unique identifier for the requested service related to the transaction.

netn.Relation.description: The specific value that represents the perceived hostility status.

affiliate.description: The force that the current force has a relation to

netn.ReleaseDistributionStruct.description: Mean and variance of the distribution of the particles or droplets in a release.

mean.description: Mean size of the released material in meters.

variance.description: Variance of the distribution of released particulates/droplets.

netn.ReleaseDynamicsStruct.description: Defines the dynamic properties of a release.

densityRatio.description: Ratio of material density relative to the atmosphere.

temperatureDifference.description: Difference in temperature between the released material and the atmosphere in Celsius.

netn.ReleaseSizeStruct.description: Defines the properties of the initial size of a release.

releaseDistribution.description: Details regarding the size of the released particles/droplets for non-gaseous materials.

sigmaArray.description: Initial Gaussian sigmas of the released puff.

netn.RemoveCheckPoint.description: Tasking of an entity to remove a checkpoint. This task removes a previously established checkpoint. The tasked entity should be within a certain distance (tolerance specified in the federation agreements) of the location of the checkpoint. If not, a separate move task should be issued first. After completion of the task, the checkpoint object should be deleted from the federation.

communicationNetworkIds.description: Optional. Reference to communication networks (NETN-COM) used to transfer tasking messages. If not provided, the task transmission should not be modelled and federates should receive and act on the task messages directly.

startWhen.description: Required. Time when the task execution should start. Use the value 00000000000000.000 to indicate that the time is undefined.

taskId.description: Required. Uniqie identifier for the task.

taskee.description: Required. Reference to the entity that will execute the task.

tasker.description: Optional. Callsign of the commander of the task. If missing, the commander is undefined.

uniqueId.description: Required. Reference to the CheckPoint to remove.

netn.TMRInteraction.description: The `TMR_Interaction` interaction class is the base for all Transfer of Modelling Responsibility interactions. It provides a single parameter used in all TMR interactions to uniquely identify TMR transactions between participating federates.

eventId.description: Required. The `EventId` is a unique reference to a specific execution of a TMR pattern. It consists of two values, a Counter and a Federate Handle. The value of this parameter is also encoded and used as the User Supplied Tag in the HLA Ownership services to allow a correlation between the TMR interactions and HLA Ownership services. The `EventId` is generated by a trigger federate or, if no trigger federate is used, directly by the requesting federate. The value of this parameter should match all related TMR pattern interactions belonging to the same Transfer of Modelling Responsibilities event.

netn.TaskModeEnum8.description: Execute task in non-concurrent mode or concurrent mode.    In the non-concurrent mode the task is placed on the task list for the entity, which serves as a waiting list. Once the task is at the head of the task list, and the currently executing task completes, it is removed from the task list and started. Using this task mode, tasks are executed one after the other.    In the concurrent mode, the task is executed concurrently with other tasks. As soon as the task is accepted for an entity, it is started. With this task mode, there is no task list involved.
